automotive-ev-battery.title: Porsche to shut down battery subsidiary Cellforce
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Porsche is closing its battery cell subsidiary Cellforce and eliminating 50 jobs as part of a broader cost-reduction strategy following its withdrawal from Rimac. The company is also discontinuing its e-bike drivetrain business and winding down software subsidiary Cetitec.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hunan Yuneng plans cathode material factory in Spain
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery materials company Hunan Yuneng announced plans to build an €800 million LFP cathode material production facility in Mérida, Spain, creating approximately 500 jobs. The facility will supply cathode materials for electric vehicle batteries, marking a significant investment in European battery supply chain localization.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Polestar posts wider Q1 losses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Geely's premium EV brand Polestar reported that its net loss more than doubled in Q1 2026 compared to the previous year despite maintaining nearly constant revenue. CEO Michael Lohscheller is focusing on cost reduction and dealer network expansion ahead of new model launches.

automotive-ev-battery.title: PPC plans 34 fast-charging sites in Greece and Romania
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Greece's Public Power Corporation will deploy 106 fast and ultra-fast charging points across 34 strategic locations in Greece and Romania along major European transport corridors. The infrastructure project aims to support EV adoption and cross-border mobility in southeastern Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 240 New Electric Buses Coming To German City
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hamburg, Germany is procuring 240 electric buses to be deployed by 2031 as part of its public transit electrification strategy. The initiative aligns with broader European efforts to achieve zero-emission public transportation, following Copenhagen's transition to a 100 percent electric bus fleet.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Uganda targets fossil-fuel free public transport by 2030
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uganda announced an ambitious strategy to electrify all public transportation by 2030, including buses and motorcycle taxis known as boda-bodas. The initiative supports the country's broader goals to develop its automotive manufacturing sector while addressing transportation emissions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ampeco teams up with Plugsurfing on roaming for eMSPs
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ampeco and Plugsurfing have partnered to offer managed roaming services for e-mobility service providers across Europe through a single Roam-OCPI integration. The collaboration provides eMSP customers access to over one million charging points in 28 European countries.

automotive-ev-battery.title: China's Electric Concrete Mixer Boom Is A Warning To Slow Heavy Truck Markets
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Battery-electric concrete mixers have rapidly transitioned from a niche segment to a major new-sales category in China within five years, demonstrating that some heavy-duty vehicles are easier to electrify than anticipated. The trend signals potential acceleration in heavy transport electrification globally.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ten more eEconic vehicles for Frankfurt
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Frankfurt's waste management company FES is adding ten Mercedes-Benz eEconic battery-electric low-floor trucks to its fleet, bringing the total number of electric waste collection vehicles to 34. The procurement reflects increasing adoption of zero-emission commercial vehicles in municipal operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Norwegian consortium develops inductive ship charger for offshore applications
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A Norwegian consortium is developing an inductive charging adapter to address challenges associated with cable-based charging of battery-electric service boats used in offshore wind farm maintenance. The technology aims to provide a practical solution for charging vessels in harsh maritime conditions.

world-news.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine to observe three-day ceasefire
world-news.summary: The United States has announced a proposed three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, amid mutual accusations of violations of separate ceasefires during Soviet victory commemorations. The development represents a significant diplomatic initiative in the ongoing conflict between the two nations.

world-news.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure'
world-news.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has accused the United States of conducting military attacks whenever diplomatic solutions emerge. The statement reflects escalating tensions between the two nations and suggests a breakdown in ongoing peace negotiations.

world-news.title: Iran war live: US expects Tehran's reply to peace deal; 'clashes' in Hormuz
world-news.summary: The United States awaits Iran's response to a proposed peace agreement as military tensions remain elevated in the Strait of Hormuz. Israel has also intensified operations in southern Lebanon, raising regional security concerns.

world-news.title: Libya's largest oil refinery halts operations during fighting
world-news.summary: Libya's Zawiya refinery, the nation's largest, has suspended operations as a precautionary measure following explosions and gunfire in the vicinity. The shutdown threatens regional energy supply and reflects ongoing instability in the country.

world-news.title: Pentagon releases video of strikes on Iranian oil tankers
world-news.summary: The Pentagon has released footage documenting U.S. military strikes against two Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. The disclosure underscores rising military confrontation between the United States and Iran in a critical global shipping corridor.

world-news.title: Hungary's new PM to be sworn in during 'regime change' party
world-news.summary: Péter Magyar, whose Tisza party achieved a landslide victory nearly a month ago, is being sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister, ending 16 years of Viktor Orbán's rule. The transition marks a significant political shift in a key European Union member state.

world-news.title: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica's new president
world-news.summary: Costa Rica's right-wing president Laura Fernandez has been inaugurated with her party holding an absolute majority in the legislature. Her administration represents a significant rightward political shift in the Central American nation.

world-news.title: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
world-news.summary: A global cyberattack has compromised the Canvas academic software platform used by thousands of educational institutions worldwide. The breach represents a significant disruption to international education systems and raises critical cybersecurity concerns.

world-news.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
world-news.summary: At least twelve countries are now linked to a hantavirus outbreak originating from the cruise ship MV Hondius, with the UN health agency confirming at least five confirmed cases. International health authorities are conducting a coordinated effort to trace and monitor potentially affected passengers globally.

world-news.title: Greece examines mystery naval drone found in Ionian Sea
world-news.summary: Greek authorities are investigating a mystery naval drone discovered in the Ionian Sea that reportedly contained explosives. Greek media suggests the device may be Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, raising concerns about military activity in Mediterranean waters.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured detailed imagery of Mars on May 3, 2026, while approaching the planet from 3 million miles away in preparation for a critical gravity assist maneuver scheduled for May 15. The gravity assist will accelerate the spacecraft and adjust its trajectory toward its primary destination, the metal-rich asteroid Psyche. This milestone marks significant progress in one of NASA's most ambitious deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: Blue Origin tests 1st moon lander ahead of lunar launch later this year
science-space.summary: Blue Origin is conducting comprehensive testing of its first lunar lander spacecraft across multiple NASA facilities to prepare for an anticipated moon landing mission later in 2026. The testing regimen is designed to validate the lander's systems and operational readiness prior to its scheduled launch. This development represents a significant milestone in commercial lunar exploration capabilities.

science-space.title: Scientists found 10,000 possible exoplanets hiding in NASA data
science-space.summary: Researchers have identified approximately 10,000 candidate exoplanets within existing NASA datasets, expanding the roster of potential worlds awaiting confirmation and further study. This discovery nearly doubles the number of known exoplanet candidates and underscores the vast amount of unprocessed astronomical data available for scientific analysis. The findings have significant implications for understanding planetary system diversity and the prevalence of worlds beyond our solar system.

science-space.title: Black holes slamming into scorching stars may be causing mysterious blue flashes in the cosmos
science-space.summary: Astrophysicists propose that Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients, powerful bright cosmic explosions, may be caused by black holes or neutron stars colliding with the universe's most massive and hottest stars. This theoretical framework offers a potential explanation for these mysterious and previously poorly understood astronomical phenomena. The research contributes to broader efforts to classify and understand transient cosmic events.

science-space.title: NASA's SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
science-space.summary: NASA and SpaceX are preparing for a mid-May launch of the Dragon spacecraft aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, carrying approximately 6,500 pounds of scientific equipment and supplies to the International Space Station. Following launch, the Dragon will autonomously dock to the forward port of the orbital outpost. The mission will deliver critical materials supporting ongoing scientific investigations aboard the space station.

science-space.title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
science-space.summary: NASA and industry partners are advancing next-generation spacecraft computing processors that build upon decades of development in radiation-hardened computing technology dating back to the Apollo Guidance Computers. These enhanced processors are essential for coordinating and executing critical spacecraft functions including guidance, navigation, and control systems. The advancement represents a significant evolution in on-board computing capability for deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: NASA Fuel Cell Tests Pave Way for Energy Storage on Moon
science-space.summary: NASA researchers are conducting extensive testing of regenerative fuel cell systems designed to provide energy storage capabilities for lunar exploration missions, utilizing nearly 270 sensors and 1,000 monitoring points during evaluation. These cylindrical fuel cell assemblies represent a critical technology for sustaining extended human operations on the lunar surface. The testing program demonstrates NASA's commitment to developing reliable power solutions for future moon bases.

science-space.title: The charred hull of Artemis 2's Orion
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft displays visible thermal damage and charring on its exterior hull following its successful return from a circumlunar flight. The damage patterns documented in post-mission imagery provide valuable engineering data on thermal protection system performance during reentry conditions. This assessment contributes to ongoing refinement of human spaceflight vehicle design for future lunar missions.

science-space.title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
science-space.summary: NASA has appointed Brian Hughes as senior director of launch operations, returning to the agency to oversee strategic direction and operational management of the organization's launch infrastructure at Kennedy Space Center. In this senior leadership position, Hughes will report directly to NASA Headquarters and maintain responsibility for enterprise-level launch operations across the agency. The appointment reflects NASA's continued focus on strengthening operational capabilities for upcoming missions.

science-space.title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
science-space.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured striking imagery of the Milky Way rising above Earth's atmospheric glow from the International Space Station on April 13, 2026, documenting the phenomenon known as airglow. Airglow occurs when atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere, energized by sunlight, release light to dissipate excess energy. The photograph provides compelling visual documentation of Earth's atmospheric physics as observed from orbital altitude.

finance-markets.title: Jane Street earned $10bn in first quarter as it doubled trading revenue
finance-markets.summary: Proprietary trading firm Jane Street reported exceptional first-quarter earnings of $10 billion, with trading revenue doubling year-over-year, cementing its position as one of Wall Street's most profitable firms. The results underscore the firm's dominance in quantitative trading and market-making activities.

finance-markets.title: While AI pumps up portfolios, some companies sound the alarm
finance-markets.summary: Concentrated gains among Big Tech companies driven by artificial intelligence enthusiasm are masking broader market weakness and financial stress at other firms. The market rally is increasingly dependent on a narrow cohort of mega-cap technology stocks, raising concerns about underlying economic health.

finance-markets.title: Markets are banking on the 'Bliss trade'
finance-markets.summary: Investors are maintaining elevated stock valuations based on expectations of sustained government support and economic resilience, a market dynamic known as the 'Bliss trade.' This reliance on state intervention underscores investor confidence in policy backstops despite underlying economic uncertainties.

finance-markets.title: Colombia Inflation Ticks Up, Fueling Case For Renewed Rate Hikes
finance-markets.summary: Colombian inflation accelerated in April, moving further away from the central bank's target and increasing the likelihood of renewed interest rate hikes after an unexpected pause last month. The uptick signals mounting inflationary pressures in the South American economy.

finance-markets.title: Private Equity Taps Junk Market for Dividends as Exits Stall
finance-markets.summary: Private equity firms are returning to the European high-yield debt market to fund dividend payments to themselves as market volatility linked to geopolitical tensions and AI uncertainty constrains their ability to complete profitable exits. The trend reflects liquidity pressures in the private equity sector.

finance-markets.title: BlackRock Readies Launch of Two Tokenized Money-Market Funds
finance-markets.summary: BlackRock is preparing to launch two money-market funds designed for investors holding stablecoins, signaling the world's largest asset manager's confidence in the growth of the digital-dollar economy. The move reflects institutional adoption of blockchain-based financial services.

finance-markets.title: Fed Sees Private Credit Redemptions as 'Manageable' Risks
finance-markets.summary: The Federal Reserve assessed that stability risks from further private credit fund redemptions appear limited and manageable, providing reassurance after several major market participants restricted investor withdrawals. The statement reflects central bank confidence in the resilience of the private credit market.

finance-markets.title: Honeywell-Backed Computing Firm Quantinuum Files for US IPO
finance-markets.summary: Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by industrial conglomerate Honeywell, filed for a U.S. initial public offering, capitalizing on heightened investor enthusiasm for quantum technology. The IPO reflects growing commercial interest in quantum computing applications.

finance-markets.title: Pakistan to Receive $1.32 Billion as IMF Board Approves Tranches
finance-markets.summary: Pakistan will receive approximately $1.32 billion following International Monetary Fund board approval of loan tranches under existing facilities, strengthening the country's economic defenses against global headwinds. The disbursement enhances Pakistan's financial stability and foreign exchange reserves.

finance-markets.title: Florida, Louisiana-Focused Insurer Safepoint Files for IPO
finance-markets.summary: Safepoint Holdings Inc., a specialty insurance underwriter focused on homeowners and commercial coverage in hurricane-prone regions, filed for an initial public offering with disclosed growth in profitability and revenue. The IPO reflects market appetite for insurance sector exposure amid rising natural disaster risks.

tech-ai.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
tech-ai.summary: Cloudflare announced its first major layoff, with CEO Matthew Prince attributing the elimination of approximately 1,100 positions to artificial intelligence efficiency gains that reduced the need for support roles. The announcement underscores how AI adoption is reshaping workforce requirements across the tech industry, even as the company achieves record revenue.

tech-ai.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
tech-ai.summary: Apple and Intel have reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to manufacture chips for Apple hardware, marking a significant reversal of Apple's 2020 transition to proprietary Apple Silicon. The partnership comes as Intel seeks a turnaround under new leadership and with government backing.

tech-ai.title: Intel's comeback story is even wilder than it seems
tech-ai.summary: Intel's stock has surged 490 percent over the past year as Wall Street bets on the chipmaker's turnaround, though analysts suggest the valuation may outpace the company's actual operational recovery. The dramatic rally reflects investor optimism about Intel's strategic initiatives and government support.

tech-ai.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
tech-ai.summary: Poland's intelligence agency has accused Russia of conducting sabotage and hacking operations against the country's military and civilian infrastructure, including water treatment facilities, highlighting a critical vulnerability also facing the United States. The incident underscores growing concerns about foreign actors targeting essential infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal, judge rules
tech-ai.summary: A federal judge ruled that the Department of Government Efficiency's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional, criticizing its use of ChatGPT to automatically identify diversity, equity, and inclusion-related programs. The 143-page decision highlights significant concerns about AI's role in government decision-making processes.

tech-ai.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
tech-ai.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride, Uber's autonomous vehicle partner, following identification of over a dozen crashes and one minor injury. The inquiry raises questions about the safety standards and oversight of autonomous vehicle technology in commercial deployment.

tech-ai.title: Here is Yarbo's promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
tech-ai.summary: Yarbo has issued a comprehensive response to security vulnerabilities in its robot lawn mowers that allowed hackers to hijack devices and access sensitive user data including GPS coordinates and Wi-Fi passwords. The company confirmed the researcher's findings and outlined a detailed remediation plan to address the critical security flaws.

tech-ai.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
tech-ai.summary: Oracle rejected severance negotiations with laid-off employees, many of whom discovered they did not qualify for WARN Act protections requiring two months' notice because the company had classified them as remote workers. The dispute raises questions about labor protections for distributed workforce members during mass layoffs.

tech-ai.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
tech-ai.summary: A former cybersecurity executive has been ordered to pay $10 million after stealing surveillance and hacking tools from his employer and selling them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker with ties to Vladimir Putin's government. The case highlights serious national security concerns regarding the illicit trade of sensitive cyber tools.

tech-ai.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
tech-ai.summary: The surge in AI data center construction is driving global conflicts over energy consumption, grid capacity, environmental impact, and community concerns, with companies pursuing ambitious projects from space-based facilities to expanding terrestrial infrastructure. The boom reflects tech industry investment in computational capacity for AI applications while raising questions about sustainability and resource allocation.

health-biotech.title: Roche to buy PathAI for $750 million
health-biotech.summary: Roche has announced the acquisition of PathAI for $750 million to accelerate its deployment of artificial intelligence in pathology and disease diagnosis. The deal represents a major investment in AI-driven diagnostic capabilities for one of the world's largest pharmaceutical companies.

health-biotech.title: Daiichi Sankyo posts 'extraordinary loss' of nearly $1B
health-biotech.summary: Daiichi Sankyo reported an extraordinary loss of approximately $950 million after overestimating demand for antibody-drug conjugates and scrapping plans for new manufacturing capacity. The writedown reflects significant miscalculation in the company's ADC strategy and third-party manufacturing commitments.

health-biotech.title: Trump reportedly plans to fire FDA Commissioner Makary
health-biotech.summary: Multiple reports indicate that FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is expected to be terminated from his position, though final plans remain subject to change. The move would create another significant leadership vacancy within the health department leadership.

health-biotech.title: Lilly, Gilead lead pharma's M&A boom
health-biotech.summary: Deal volume in the pharmaceutical industry is substantially outpacing prior-year levels in 2026, with major activity concentrated in cancer and autoimmune disease therapeutics. Leading dealmakers Eli Lilly and Gilead Sciences are driving significant merger and acquisition activity.

health-biotech.title: Capricor Therapeutics accuses Nippon Shinyaku of slow-walking plans on Duchenne drug
health-biotech.summary: Capricor Therapeutics has filed a lawsuit against Nippon Shinyaku alleging mismanagement of the commercialization and distribution agreement for deramiocel, a cell therapy candidate for Duchenne muscular dystrophy. The dispute centers on pricing strategy and launch preparation delays.

health-biotech.title: FDA delays Leqembi decision; Artiva raises $300M; Pharvaris prices offering
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has extended its review timeline for the subcutaneous formulation of Leqembi, pushing the decision date to August 24 after requesting additional information from Biogen and Eisai. The delay signals continued regulatory scrutiny of the Alzheimer's disease treatment.

health-biotech.title: Odyssey raises more than $300M as biotech goes public
health-biotech.summary: Odyssey Therapeutics completed an initial public offering, raising $279 million and selling 15.5 million shares on the Nasdaq, after successfully rebounding from a withdrawn filing the previous year. The autoimmune disease drugmaker's IPO adds to recent momentum in biotech capital markets.

health-biotech.title: FDA to reevaluate spurned cell therapy; Under-the-skin Leqembi review extended
health-biotech.summary: Pierre Fabre announced alignment with FDA regulators on a revised path forward for a twice-rejected cell therapy treatment, signaling renewed regulatory engagement. In related news, leadership transitions occurred at Argenx while Blackstone committed $250 million in biotech investment.

health-biotech.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for the Canary Islands
health-biotech.summary: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive over 140 passengers and crew members from a cruise ship affected by hantavirus outbreak at the Canary Islands. The public health response represents a containment effort for a potentially serious infectious disease situation.

health-biotech.title: 'Pharma, not really': Top young AI talent shuns careers at big drugmakers
health-biotech.summary: Leading AI researchers and computational scientists, including postdoctoral fellows from top institutions like MIT and Harvard Medical School, are increasingly choosing not to pursue careers at large pharmaceutical companies. The trend suggests potential recruitment challenges for traditional pharma in the competitive AI talent market.

crypto-web3.title: Senate Banking Committee plans to hold key market structure hearing on Thursday
crypto-web3.summary: The Senate Banking Committee announced it will hold a markup hearing for the Clarity Act on March 14, advancing sweeping crypto legislation. This marks a critical legislative milestone following recent bipartisan compromise on stablecoin provisions that had previously stalled progress.

crypto-web3.title: SEC chair Atkins signals new rules for onchain markets, AI-driven finance
crypto-web3.summary: SEC Chair Paul Atkins announced plans for new regulatory rules governing onchain market infrastructure and AI-powered financial systems. The signaling of support for blockchain-based markets and automated settlement frameworks represents a significant policy shift for institutional digital asset adoption.

crypto-web3.title: 'Shifted the vibes:' Stablecoin deal revives crypto bill despite lingering ethics disputes
crypto-web3.summary: Lawmakers reached a compromise on stablecoin provisions that had become a major obstacle to passing comprehensive crypto legislation. Industry observers report renewed momentum following the breakthrough, though some ethical concerns remain unresolved.

crypto-web3.title: Kraken parent goes for the OCC charter in bid to become a federal crypto bank
crypto-web3.summary: Payward, Kraken's parent company, has applied for a national OCC trust charter to establish federally regulated digital asset banking infrastructure. The application builds on existing state and federal regulatory approvals and follows similar charter applications by Ripple and Coinbase.

crypto-web3.title: Stablecoins have their 'permission slip.' Now comes the hard part.
crypto-web3.summary: Industry executives from MoonPay, Ripple, and Paxos acknowledged that regulatory clarity has accelerated stablecoin adoption, but significant infrastructure, privacy, and distribution challenges remain. The discussion highlighted the gap between regulatory approval and practical market implementation.

crypto-web3.title: ECB's Lagarde's digital euro warning: Why Europe shouldn't just copy the U.S. stablecoin model
crypto-web3.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned that dominant stablecoins like Tether and USDC, representing a $310 billion market, pose systemic financial stability risks and could transmit market stress during volatility. Lagarde advocated for Europe to develop distinct digital currency frameworks independent of private stablecoin models.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase rebounds as altcoins surge with bitcoin holding above $80,000
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase and digital asset infrastructure stocks rallied as cryptocurrency markets showed strength with Bitcoin maintaining levels above $80,000. The market movement coincided with SEC Chair Atkins' supportive signals regarding onchain finance regulation.

crypto-web3.title: AI agents fueled a frenzy of startup building at the Consensus Miami EasyA hackathon
crypto-web3.summary: Nearly 1,000 developers from major technology companies including Microsoft and Google competed at the Consensus Miami hackathon, with the majority focused on building AI agent applications across blockchain ecosystems including Base and Solana. The event demonstrated significant developer interest at the intersection of artificial intelligence and decentralized infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: SEC weighs new rulemaking for onchain market structures and software applications
crypto-web3.summary: SEC Chair Paul Atkins indicated the agency plans to clarify how existing regulatory frameworks apply to blockchain-based market infrastructure and software applications. The anticipated rulemaking addresses ambiguities in the regulatory treatment of decentralized systems and automated protocols.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase bulls point to crypto legislation and stablecoins after earnings miss
crypto-web3.summary: Analysts suggested that Coinbase's long-term growth trajectory may depend more on favorable crypto legislation and stablecoin adoption than near-term trading volume recovery following the company's earnings shortfall. The assessment reflects expectations that regulatory progress will be a primary growth catalyst for institutional digital asset platforms.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group has launched a data extortion attack targeting Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes and coursework across numerous U.S. school districts and universities. The attackers defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
cybersecurity.summary: ShinyHunters has claimed responsibility for a second attack against Instructure, the company behind the widely-used Canvas learning management system. The incident puts personally identifiable information of hundreds of millions of individuals at risk and highlights the edtech company's struggle to regain control from attackers.

cybersecurity.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have discovered a previously undocumented Linux implant called Quasar Linux RAT that targets developers' systems to facilitate credential harvesting, keylogging, and post-compromise functionality across the software supply chain. The malware enables attackers to establish persistent footholds and harvest DevOps credentials for broader infrastructure compromise.

cybersecurity.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
cybersecurity.summary: Threat hunters have identified TCLBANKER, a previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan capable of targeting 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms through WhatsApp and Outlook worm propagation. The malware, tracked as REF3076, represents a major update to the Maverick trojan and poses significant risks to financial sector users.

cybersecurity.title: Linux Kernel Dirty Frag LPE Exploit Enables Root Access Across Major Distributions
cybersecurity.summary: A new unpatched local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel, dubbed Dirty Frag, has been disclosed as a successor to the recently exploited Copy Fail vulnerability. The vulnerability enables root access across major Linux distributions and has been reported to kernel maintainers for remediation.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers discovered 28 fraudulent applications on the Google Play Store that falsely claimed to provide call history access while instead tricking users into paid subscriptions that delivered only fake data. The malicious apps accumulated over 7.3 million downloads, causing direct financial losses to victims.

cybersecurity.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have disclosed PamDOORa, a new Linux backdoor being sold on Russian cybercrime forums for $1,600 that leverages Pluggable Authentication Modules to enable persistent SSH access and credential theft. The post-exploitation toolkit uses magic password and TCP port combinations to maintain clandestine system access.

cybersecurity.title: VoidStealer Malware Darts Past Google Chrome's Encryption
cybersecurity.summary: Threat researchers have identified additional bypass techniques used by the VoidStealer trojan to circumvent Google Chrome's App-Bound Encryption, further enabling infostealer malware to extract sensitive user data. The discovery underscores ongoing vulnerabilities in browser-based encryption mechanisms.

cybersecurity.title: After Replacing TeamPCP Malware, 'PCPJack' Steals Cloud Secrets
cybersecurity.summary: PCPJack, a successor to TeamPCP malware, employs innovative tactics including the use of parquet files for stealthy target discovery while canvassing multiple cloud environments to steal sensitive cloud credentials and secrets. The malware demonstrates sophisticated evasion techniques in its reconnaissance operations.

cybersecurity.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers are exploiting Windows Phone Link, the Windows-based bridge connecting PCs to smartphones, by deploying the CloudZ RAT and new plugin Pheno to intercept text messages and circumvent two-factor authentication. The attack methodology is difficult to detect and poses significant risks to user account security.

startups-vc.title: Billion-Dollar AI Rounds Push April To Third-Highest Startup Funding Month In A Year
startups-vc.summary: Global venture funding reached $56 billion in April 2026, marking the third-largest monthly funding total in the past year, with growth driven primarily by large AI-focused funding rounds. The data reflects a 100% year-over-year increase, demonstrating continued investor appetite for artificial intelligence companies.

startups-vc.title: Blitzy Raises $200M At $1.4B Valuation For Autonomous Software Development
startups-vc.summary: Blitzy, a Cambridge-based autonomous software development startup, raised $200 million in funding at a $1.4 billion valuation, bringing its total funding to over $204 million. The round reflects strong investor confidence in AI-powered developer tools designed to streamline enterprise coding processes.

startups-vc.title: The "people's airline" and the enterprise AI gold rush
startups-vc.summary: Enterprise AI has become a major acquisition target, with major companies including Anthropic, OpenAI, and SAP making significant moves including a $1 billion investment by SAP in German AI startup Prior Labs. The trend demonstrates that startups building enterprise tools are increasingly viewed as strategic acquisition opportunities by larger technology vendors.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Defense Tech Leads With Multiple Large Deals, Topped By $600M For Space Security Startup True Anomaly
startups-vc.summary: Defense technology startups dominated venture funding this week, led by a $600 million raise for space security company True Anomaly. Other significant rounds were announced for startups applying AI across fintech, marketing, customer service, healthcare, and developer tools sectors.

startups-vc.title: The B2B Reacceleration Is Real, But Uneven. Twilio, Atlassian, Datadog, Cloudflare, and Palantir Just Proved It.
startups-vc.summary: Major B2B software companies demonstrated renewed growth momentum, with Twilio reaching its highest growth in three years at 20%+ acceleration and Atlassian jumping to 32% growth, while Datadog reported its first-ever $1 billion quarter. The results indicate a broader reacceleration in the B2B market, though growth remains uneven across the sector.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Sales And Marketing Gets An AI Makeover
startups-vc.summary: Sales, marketing, and CRM companies have raised approximately $2.7 billion globally in seed through growth-stage funding so far in 2026. The sector snapshot highlights the significant investment momentum in AI-powered go-to-market tools.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced that Harshita Arora has joined as a General Partner, bringing deep fintech and infrastructure expertise along with a track record of founding companies since her teenage years. The appointment reflects YC's continued focus on strengthening its investment team with experienced operators.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Enterprise AI, Space Tech And Biotech Top The Ranks
startups-vc.summary: Enterprise AI, space technology, and biotech companies dominated the week's largest funding rounds, continuing the trend of billion-dollar investments in emerging technologies. The data reflects investor focus on high-impact sectors with significant market potential.

startups-vc.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
startups-vc.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature expert VCs discussing Series A funding strategies and timelines for 2027, highlighting that many founders may already be behind on their fundraising schedules. The session will be held at San Francisco's Moscone West on October 13-15.

startups-vc.title: DAU, WAU and MAU Are the New Lighthouse Metric in B2B + AI. Harvey's a Great Case Study.
startups-vc.summary: Daily, weekly, and monthly active users have become critical engagement metrics for B2B software companies in the AI era, shifting focus from traditional B2B metrics like ARR and NRR. The shift reflects how AI-powered products are changing customer engagement patterns and how companies should measure product-market fit.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 8 May 2026: EU eyes fossil-fuel exemptions | Wind and solar save UK '£1.7bn' | Amazon 'tipping point'
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major climate developments including the EU's consideration of fossil-fuel exemptions in climate policy, quantified savings from UK wind and solar deployment worth £1.7 billion, and analysis of Amazon rainforest tipping points. The roundup synthesizes key policy, technology, and environmental trends shaping the global energy transition.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 1 May 2026: Countries chart path away from fossil fuels | China's clean-tech surge | Global forest loss slows
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief reports on international progress in fossil fuel transition, China's accelerating clean technology deployment, and a global slowdown in forest loss rates. The digest highlights major policy shifts and technological advances driving decarbonization globally.

energy-climate.title: Germany risks €45 billion taxpayer hit on hydrogen overbuild, warns IEEFA
energy-climate.summary: The Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis warns that Germany's hydrogen infrastructure strategy could leave taxpayers liable for €34.7 billion in unrecovered costs by 2055, nearly doubling the commonly cited €19.8 billion construction budget. The analysis raises significant questions about the financial viability and cost management of Germany's hydrogen expansion plans.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: How countries got the global 'net-zero' shipping deal 'back on track'
energy-climate.summary: Nations have renewed their commitment to developing a comprehensive framework for reducing global shipping emissions after previous negotiations stalled. The agreement represents a critical step toward decarbonizing international maritime transport, a sector responsible for significant global carbon emissions.

energy-climate.title: EU ban on Chinese inverters sparks strong response from Beijing
energy-climate.summary: China has protested the European Union's restriction on Chinese solar inverters in EU-funded projects, warning of potential damage to trade relations and Europe's clean energy transition. The trade dispute highlights mounting tensions over renewable energy supply chains and manufacturing standards.

energy-climate.title: Spain accelerates rollout of energy communities
energy-climate.summary: Spain has expanded its energy community program to 262 projects with €108.4 million in EU funding, deploying 175.3 MW of solar capacity while engaging over 111,000 citizens. The acceleration demonstrates community-led renewable energy models delivering both decarbonization and social participation at scale.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: What the UK car industry is not saying about EV targets
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief investigates UK automotive industry claims regarding electric vehicle demand and transition timelines, identifying gaps in their public statements about EV adoption capability. The analysis challenges industry narratives often used to justify delays in meeting government electrification targets.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: Nel ASA launches new pressurized alkaline electrolyzer
energy-climate.summary: Nel ASA has released a next-generation pressurized alkaline electrolyzer capable of achieving turnkey costs below $1,450 per kilowatt for 25 MW installations, with further cost reductions anticipated at larger scales. The technological advancement addresses a critical cost barrier for green hydrogen production and commercialization.

energy-climate.title: Kalyon PV begins production at new TOPCon solar cell factory in Turkey
energy-climate.summary: Kalyon PV has commenced operations at a new 1 gigawatt solar cell manufacturing facility in Ankara, increasing the company's total production capacity to 2.1 GW. The expansion strengthens regional solar manufacturing capacity outside traditional Asian production centers.

energy-climate.title: New electrolyte tech enables stable operation of high-voltage sodium-ion batteries
energy-climate.summary: U.S. researchers have developed an advanced electrolyte formulation that optimizes sodium-ion transport and improves electrode-electrolyte stability in high-voltage sodium-ion batteries. The innovation extends battery cycle life and represents progress toward cost-effective alternatives to lithium-ion technology for grid storage applications.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong schools record net student rise for 2 years straight
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong schools have recorded net student rises for two consecutive years, with the current academic year seeing an increase of about 7,200 pupils, a surge that an industry leader has attributed to the influx of dependants of admitted talent.
The Education Bureau published enrolment figures on its website on April 28. The data covers all government, aided, private, international and direct subsidy scheme schools at the time of the annual headcount exercise in September 2025.
To determine the..

asia-pacific.title: Investors have worries about Trump’s pick for Fed chair. Should they?
asia-pacific.summary:
In an extraordinary break from the diplomatic restraint typical of central banks, a dozen leaders of the world’s foremost monetary institutions issued a joint statement in January declaring their “full solidarity” with the US Federal Reserve and its embattled chair, Jerome Powell.
“The independence of central banks is a cornerstone of price, financial and economic stability in the interest of the citizens that we serve,” they wrote.
The move was intended to shore up the separation of monetary...

asia-pacific.title: China boy, 4, survives 11-floor fall after climbing onto window to see if parents were coming home
asia-pacific.summary:
A four-year-old boy miraculously survived after falling from the window of his 11th-floor home.
After the boy gained consciousness, he said in tears to his mother that he had climbed onto the window because he was missing her so much that he wanted to see if she was coming home.
The boy’s experience went viral online after his experience was reported by the Haibao News in early May.
The incident unfolded on April 11 when the boy, nicknamed Xiaoming, was left alone in his home in Dalian, Liaoning

asia-pacific.title: Mexico to end school year 40 days early for World Cup amid heat wave
asia-pacific.summary:
Mexican authorities have announced that the school year will end over a month early as the country gears up to host World Cup matches, prompting broad backlash.
Education Secretary Mario Delgado said the decision to end the school year 40 days early was made in part due to a heatwave affecting several states.
“We’re going to end the school year on June 5 because many states are already experiencing high temperatures, and there’s also the issue of the World Cup,” he said at an event on Friday in.

asia-pacific.title: Singapore shines as stable investment oasis amid global storms: ‘very appetising’
asia-pacific.summary:
As global markets are roiled by geopolitical uncertainty and the energy fallout from war in the Middle East, a tiny speck in Southeast Asia has emerged as an oasis of stability – at least in terms of where the well-heeled can park their money.
Giig Tanaporn, CEO and founder of business and wealth consultancy Unique Prime Group, has observed that high-net-worth clients, especially from the Middle East and South Korea, have been increasingly choosing Singapore for its regulatory framework,...

asia-pacific.title: How a China-backed highway may turn landlocked Laos into a trade gateway
asia-pacific.summary:
China has moved early to help Laos plan its longest highway, the latest project in Beijing’s push to expand transport connectivity in Southeast Asia.
The initiative underscores Beijing’s broader effort to strengthen its economic footprint in the region through roads and railway infrastructure, as it seeks alternative trade routes amid a fractious relationship with the United States.
Laos is conducting route surveys for an expressway linking the capital Vientiane to its northeastern border with..

asia-pacific.title: Trump appeals latest court loss on tariffs as chaos and uncertainty amp up
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump filed an appeal on Friday after the latest legal setback to his signature tariff policy, piling on more chaos and global uncertainty and setting up a new tussle over billions of US dollars in refunds.
The appeal follows a decision on Thursday by the US Court of International Trade that invalidated his use of a Section 122 trade provision that saw him slap 10 per cent import taxes on nearly every country on earth. In its ruling, the court found that he failed to meet the

asia-pacific.title: Latest US-China rivalry combines undersea dominance with a race to riches
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s landmark visit to China comes as the Iran war disrupts global energy supplies, fuels economic uncertainty and adds fresh strain to Washington-Beijing ties. In the latest part of a series examining how rivalry, interdependence and geopolitical crises are reshaping the relationship between the two powers, we look at their make-or-break contest unfolding beneath the ocean surface.
Last year’s military blockbuster Operation Hadal was China’s first high-budget film to...

asia-pacific.title: Thanks to Trump, the gloves are off. There may be no new global order
asia-pacific.summary:
The old order is dead. We just don’t know what will replace it. As Henry Kissinger reminded us in his 2014 book World Order, “no truly global order has ever existed”. After US President Donald Trump’s erratic actions, the gloves are off. American comedians and Iranian Lego cartoons tell us all we need to know about the demise of the old order.
If the unipolar order is not viable, and America is abandoning the multilateral order and the rules of the game it created after World War II, what are...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Almost zero’ chance Dutch man got hantavirus in Argentina’s Ushuaia, official says
asia-pacific.summary:
There is an “almost zero” chance that the Dutch man linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship contracted the disease in the Argentine port of Ushuaia, a provincial health official said on Friday.
Juan Petrina, director of epidemiology for Tierra del Fuego province, where Ushuaia is located, told reporters that his assessment was based on the virus’s incubation period, among other factors.
The Dutch man and his wife, both of whom died of the virus, boarded the Hondius in...
